title: manulea pusilla
intro: Manulea pusilla, also known as dwarf manulea, is a small shrub native to South Africa that belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Manulea pusilla include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Manulea pusilla is typically prepared as a herbal infusion by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, and it can also be used as a tincture or added to ointments and creams for topical application.
